description: Music: organized sound, with or without a voice in it. Third member of the 'meli-' family after 'melira' (song) and 'meliho' (sing); music is the family's widest word, holding the drum and the flute where the others hold only the throat.
sound_symbolism: The family signature 'me'-'li' opens into breath-light 'phe': the sung line handed to instruments.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'mia meliphe sheluo.' (I listen to music.) 'meliphe siora loa.' (Music gives joy.)

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Music was participatory before it was professional: work songs, lullabies, the fiddle at the wedding. Everyone's hands remember an instrument.
